mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals strengthening labor demand and rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which increases corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows month-over-month growth below 1.0%, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal reverses direction during monetary easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with a declining unemployment rate create a favorable liquidity and earnings environment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0%, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters meeting the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low inflation, a sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening months, or the signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.
